title: unifree
text: unifree Free the memory that was allocated by a call to uprmgets or uuf2str . Explain this code int unifree (UHACThAny, UVOID*pData); Copy Parameters hAny Handle of the operation or entity this data belongs to pData address of the allocated data Return Values Table 1. Values returned by unifree Value Meaning 0 Success. The memory was successfully freed. -165 The supplied handle is not valid. Use Library: ucall Description unifree should be called to release memory that was allocated by a call to uprmgets or uuf2str . These two functions allocate memory internally and return a pointer by reference. As customary with functions like this, the calling program is responsible for freeing this data. Not doing so can lead to memory leaks in your program. Example: uuf2str, unifree, and ustr2uf The following code calls uuf2str to retrieve string data from an output parameter of one service. It then modifies the string and calls ustr2uf to pass it as an input parameter to another service. Note that, because we want to access and modify the string, we cannot use the detach/attach mechanism here. The operations OP1 and OP2 are both located in the service TESTSVC, and are defined as follows: Explain this code operation op1 params string msg : inout; endparams msg = "the quick brow fox jumps over the lazy dog" end operation op2 params string msg : in; endparams putmess msg end Copy And here is the 3GL routine: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <ctype.h> void get_and_put () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UCHAR* pMsg; /* Pointer to string parameter data */ UCHAR* p; /* Work pointer */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ /* Instantiate service TESTSVC */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"OP1", 0, &hOp1); /* Instantiate operation OP1 */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"OP2", 0, &hOp2); /* Instantiate operation OP1 */ uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation OP1 */ uuf2str(hOp1, 1, &pMsg); /* Get a pointer to the string */ for ( p=pMsg; *p; p++ ) *p = toupper(*p);/* Convert string to uppercase*/ ustr2uf(hOp2, 1, pMsg); /* Copy string to OP2 input parameter */ uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation OP2 */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ unifree (hOp1, pMsg); /* Free the string data */ ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free OP1 operation handle */ ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free OP2 operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy Related Links uprmgets Return Uniface-formatted data from a basic parameter or entity field. uuf2str Get a pointer to the string data in a basic parameter or entity field.
